article_title: ULA Confirms Successful GEM 63XL Static Fire as Vulcan SRB Investigation Drags On and Space Force Pause Holds

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean space-aerospace submission. Every specific verified verbatim: May 14 ULA announcement, April 15 static fire date, Graphite Epoxy Motor (GEM) 63XL designation, Northrop Grumman test execution, 'nozzle design enhancements' + 'advanced propellant' framing verbatim, Wentz title transition (VP Atlas & Vulcan Programs → VP Government & Commercial Programs), full Wentz quote 'asymmetric thrust... BE-4s and our avionics system gimbaled... arrest that roll' verbatim, USSF-87 February 12 2026 launch date, 'significant performance anomaly' verbatim, 'two spy satellites' / 'geosynchronous orbit' verbatim, Col. Eric Zarybnisky + 'Assured Access to Space' (AATS) office, Feb. 25 Warfare Symposium dateline, Zarybnisky 'work through this anomaly' + 'many-months' quotes verbatim, 'more than two dozen' national security launches booked, 'Vulcan's going to get back to flight by the end of the year' Wentz quote, Amazon as likely return-to-flight customer, Leo Atlas 07 (LA-07) NET May 22, LV-01 stacked, debris 'in water that was likely too deep for them to recover', 2024 'manufacturing defect' nozzle history + Tory Bruno attribution + March 2025 Space Force certification.

source-0.status: Spaceflight Now primary May 14 coverage. Verifies April 15 static fire, GEM 63XL designation, nozzle-design-enhancements + advanced-propellant framing, Wentz VP Government & Commercial Programs title, full asymmetric-thrust quote verbatim, 'end of the year' return-to-flight projection, Amazon LV-01 + LA-07 May 22 manifest detail, water-debris-recovery framing.

source-1.status: Space.com Space Force pause coverage. Verifies Col. Zarybnisky, Feb. 25 Warfare Symposium dateline, 'work through this anomaly... not be launching Vulcan missions' verbatim, 'many-months process' verbatim, two-dozen national security launch backlog, 2024 manufacturing-defect framing.

source-2.status: Space.com USSF-87 launch coverage. Verifies Feb. 12 launch date, 'two spy satellites' framing, Wentz original VP Atlas and Vulcan Programs title, 'significant performance anomaly' + 'delivered the spacecraft directly to geosynchronous orbit' Wentz statement verbatim, March 2025 Space Force certification.

source-3.status: Spaceflight Now historical (March 27 2025) Vulcan certification coverage. Verifies: 'Assured Access to Space (AATS)' office, Tory Bruno attribution + 'manufacturing defect in one of the internal parts of the nozzle' framing, GEM 63XL burn-through history during the second certification flight.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What We Know / Context: The October 2024 Echo / What We Don't Know). Technical, precise, neutral. Length appropriate for News (813 words). All inline citations point to the source that actually contains the claim — clean per Rule 1.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the May 14 GEM 63XL static fire and the ongoing Vulcan-pause status. No archive collision.

content_preview.title: ULA Confirms Successful GEM 63XL Static Fire as Vulcan SRB Investigation Drags On and Space Force Pause Holds
content_preview.summary: ULA says Northrop Grumman ran a successful GEM 63XL solid rocket booster static fire on April 15, but the Vulcan rocket remains grounded for national security launches three months after a nozzle anomaly on USSF-87, with Amazon expected to anchor a return-to-flight by year-end.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

United Launch Alliance said on May 14 that Northrop Grumman performed a successful static fire of a Graphite Epoxy Motor (GEM) 63XL Solid Rocket Booster on April 15, a key milestone in clearing the Vulcan rocket to fly again after a nozzle anomaly on the USSF-87 mission grounded the vehicle for national security launches. According to [Spaceflight Now](https://spaceflightnow.com/2026/05/14/ula-confirms-successful-solid-rocket-booster-test-as-vulcan-anomaly-investigation-continues/),...
